sprint: Click Empty Space Anywhere in Creator Deselects
task: Direct product feedback, closing out the Unified Layer Ordering work and starting a new phase of customizations: 'any click in empty space anywhere in creator should get us out from any selected state. that is if apple is selected right now and i click in empty space in center pane, it should get deselected. this will ensure natural usage tendencies are respected.' Investigation confirmed a genuinely separate gap, not a duplicate of existing logic: js/app.js's previewCanvas mousedown handler already deselects when a click lands on empty CANVAS pixels via hit-testing -- pre-existing, unchanged. But .preview-area (the center pane) has real, separate clickable DOM space around the canvas itself -- the flex gutter .preview-wrapper uses to center a fluid-sized canvas, and the Object Strip's own background beneath it -- neither ever had a listener, so a click there silently did nothing, exactly the reported symptom. Fixed with one new mousedown listener on .preview-area, guarded by e.target.closest('#previewCanvas, .object-card') so it never fires for a click the canvas's own hit-testing already handles or for an Object Strip card click (which already selects/deselects via its own routing) -- only a genuine empty-background click reaches it, clearing both selection channels the same way the canvas's own fallback does. Verified via a new Playwright test: a selected sticker is confirmed deselected by a real click landing inside the pane but outside the canvas; a subsequent Object Strip card click is confirmed to still work correctly and is unaffected. Full regression across the entire existing Creator/Builder/Card-Platform Playwright suite (22 scripts) and both goldenBuild.js suites passes unchanged, zero console errors. See CLAUDE.md's matching entry and docs/CREATOR_PAGE_RUNTIME.md for the full account.
